summary_answer: Yes, a doctor can operate without consent in emergency situations legally if it is impossible to obtain consent.
detailed_answer: Under Section 30 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023, a medical procedure can be performed without consent if it is done in good faith for the benefit of a person, and if circumstances make it impossible for that person to signify consent. This includes situations where the person is incapable of giving consent or there is no guardian available to provide consent in time. Additionally, Section 28 addresses situations where consent may be given under fear or misconception, which can also apply in emergency contexts. However, there is medium uncertainty regarding the specific conditions that must be met in each case.
final_answer: Applying structured reasoning over the retrieved evidence, Section 28 (Consent known to be given under fear or misconception.) is the condition-matched applicable provision.
